content: Decomposing a creative-production domain into 10 sequential phases plus 4 cross-cutting concerns (14 total subdomains) produces a natural 1:1 mapping between subdomains and coordinators for the sequential phases, while cross-cutting concerns map to shared infrastructure. This decomposition was stable — subdomain coverage was clean from cycle 1 with no gaps discovered across 4 gap-hunting cycles, suggesting the initial domain scan correctly identified all subdomains. The 14-subdomain count is likely a good target for similar creative-pipeline domains.
sourceEvidence: 14 subdomains: 10 sequential (user-guide-and-input, concept-development, worldbuilding, character-development, plotting-and-outlining, prose-style-calibration, chapter-drafting, revision-and-editing, beta-reading-simulation, polish-and-delivery) + 4 cross-cutting (adversarial-consistency, series-knowledge-management, continuity-tracking, craft-knowledge-systems). gap-report Category 1: 'All 14 subdomains covered. Clean since cycle 1.'

history.reason: Cold start — first observation from initial factory run
